blastmini.cli module

Command-line interface for blastmini.

This module provides a unified CLI for all blastmini functionality, including index building, sequence searching, and result formatting.

Usage:

blastmini build -i database.fa -k 11 -o index.json blastmini search -q query.fa -d index.json --top 10 -o results.tsv blastmini stats -i index.json blastmini view -r results.tsv --format text

blastmini.cli.cmd_build(args: Namespace) → int[源代码]

Build k-mer index from FASTA file.

blastmini.cli.cmd_info(args: Namespace) → int[源代码]

Display package information.

blastmini.cli.cmd_search(args: Namespace) → int[源代码]

Search query sequences against indexed database.

blastmini.cli.cmd_significance(args: Namespace) → int[源代码]

Estimate statistical significance of hits.

blastmini.cli.cmd_stats(args: Namespace) → int[源代码]

Display index statistics.

blastmini.cli.cmd_view(args: Namespace) → int[源代码]

View and visualize search results.

blastmini.cli.create_parser() → ArgumentParser[源代码]

Create the command-line argument parser.

blastmini.cli.format_duration(seconds: float) → str[源代码]

Format duration in human-readable format.

blastmini.cli.main() → int[源代码]
blastmini.cli.print_error(message: str) → None[源代码]

Print error message to stderr.

blastmini.cli.print_info(message: str) → None[源代码]

Print info message to stderr.

blastmini.cli.print_success(message: str) → None[源代码]

Print success message to stderr.

blastmini.cli.validate_fasta_file(filepath: str) → bool[源代码]

Validate that a FASTA file exists and is readable.

blastmini.extension module

Seed extension module for blastmini.

This module implements the X-dropoff extension algorithm, which takes seed matches and extends them bidirectionally to find high-scoring segment pairs (HSPs). The extension phase is where the actual alignment is generated from the seed positions.

Key features:

  • Bidirectional X-dropoff extension

  • Ungapped extension (simplified BLAST)

  • Support for both same-strand and reverse-complement extensions

  • Scoring with match/mismatch matrices

  • Extension statistics and quality tracking

  • Parallel extension support for multiple seeds

class blastmini.extension.ExtensionResult(query_id: str, subject_id: str, query_start: int, query_end: int, subject_start: int, subject_end: int, score: int = 0, identity: int = 0, mismatches: int = 0, alignment_length: int = 0, query_alignment: str = '', subject_alignment: str = '', strand: str = '+', seed_origin: Seed | None = None, max_score_during_extension: int = 0, extension_steps: int = 0)[源代码]

基类：object

Result of a seed extension operation.

This class stores the complete information about an extended alignment, including the aligned sequences, scores, and coordinates.

query_id

Query sequence identifier.

Type:

str

subject_id

Subject sequence identifier.

Type:

str

query_start

Start position in query (0-based, inclusive).

Type:

int

query_end

End position in query (0-based, exclusive).

Type:

int

subject_start

Start position in subject (0-based, inclusive).

Type:

int

subject_end

End position in subject (0-based, exclusive).

Type:

int

score

Total alignment score.

Type:

int

identity

Number of identical matches.

Type:

int

mismatches

Number of mismatches.

Type:

int

alignment_length

Total alignment length.

Type:

int

query_alignment

Aligned query sequence (with gaps).

Type:

str

subject_alignment

Aligned subject sequence (with gaps).

Type:

str

strand

'+' for same strand, '-' for reverse complement.

Type:

str

seed_origin

The seed that initiated this extension.

Type:

blastmini.seeding.Seed | None

max_score_during_extension

Peak score during extension.

Type:

int

extension_steps

Number of steps taken during extension.

Type:

int

示例

>>> result = ExtensionResult("Q1", "S1", 10, 50, 100, 140)
>>> print(f"Score: {result.score}, Identity: {result.identity}/{result.alignment_length}")
alignment_length: int = 0
extension_steps: int = 0
identity: int = 0
property identity_percent: float

Calculate identity percentage.

max_score_during_extension: int = 0
mismatches: int = 0
query_alignment: str = ''
query_end: int
query_id: str
property query_sequence: str

Get the query sequence without gaps.

query_start: int
score: int = 0
seed_origin: Seed | None = None
strand: str = '+'
subject_alignment: str = ''
subject_end: int
subject_id: str
property subject_sequence: str

Get the subject sequence without gaps.

subject_start: int
to_hit() → Hit[源代码]

Convert extension result to a Hit object for output.

class blastmini.extension.ExtensionStats(total_extensions: int = 0, successful_extensions: int = 0, failed_extensions: int = 0, total_extension_steps: int = 0, average_extension_length: float = 0.0, max_extension_length: int = 0, min_score: int = 0, max_score: int = 0, total_time_ms: float = 0.0)[源代码]

基类：object

Statistics about the extension process.

Tracks performance and quality metrics for extension operations.

total_extensions

Number of extension attempts.

Type:

int

successful_extensions

Number of successful extensions.

Type:

int

failed_extensions

Number of failed extensions.

Type:

int

total_extension_steps

Total steps across all extensions.

Type:

int

average_extension_length

Average length of successful extensions.

Type:

float

max_extension_length

Maximum extension length achieved.

Type:

int

min_score

Minimum score achieved.

Type:

int

max_score

Maximum score achieved.

Type:

int

total_time_ms

Total time spent in milliseconds.

Type:

float

average_extension_length: float = 0.0
failed_extensions: int = 0
max_extension_length: int = 0
max_score: int = 0
min_score: int = 0
record_extension(result: ExtensionResult) → None[源代码]

Record a single extension result.

successful_extensions: int = 0
total_extension_steps: int = 0
total_extensions: int = 0
total_time_ms: float = 0.0
class blastmini.extension.SeedExtender(config: AlignmentConfig | None = None, min_extension_score: int = 10, track_stats: bool = False)[源代码]

基类：object

Extends seeds to generate high-scoring segment pairs (HSPs).

This class implements the X-dropoff extension algorithm for both same-strand and reverse-complement alignments.

config

Alignment configuration parameters.

match_score

Score for matching nucleotides.

mismatch_penalty

Penalty for mismatching nucleotides.

x_dropoff

X-dropoff threshold for extension termination.

gap_open_penalty

Penalty for opening a gap (for future gapped extension).

gap_extend_penalty

Penalty for extending a gap (for future gapped extension).

min_extension_score

Minimum score for a valid extension.

stats

Extension statistics tracker.

示例

>>> from blastmini.index import build_index_from_fasta
>>> from blastmini.seeding import SeedFinder
>>> idx = build_index_from_fasta("database.fa", k=11)
>>> finder = SeedFinder(idx)
>>> extender = SeedExtender()
>>> query = SequenceRecord("Q1", "ATCGATCGATCG")
>>> seeds = finder.find_seeds(query)
>>> results = extender.extend_seeds(query, seeds)
estimate_extension_time(num_seeds: int) → float[源代码]

Estimate the time needed for extension.

参数:

num_seeds -- Number of seeds to extend.

返回:

Estimated time in seconds.

extend_clusters(query: SequenceRecord | str, clusters: List[SeedCluster], subject_sequences: Dict[str, str], max_results: int | None = None) → List[ExtensionResult][源代码]

Extend seed clusters and return the best results.

This method extends seeds from clusters, giving priority to clusters with more seeds (higher confidence).

参数:

  • query -- Query sequence or SequenceRecord.

  • clusters -- List of SeedCluster objects.

  • subject_sequences -- Dictionary mapping subject ID to sequence.

  • max_results -- Maximum number of results to return.

返回:

List of ExtensionResult objects sorted by score.

extend_seed(query: SequenceRecord | str, seed: Seed, subject_sequences: Dict[str, str]) → ExtensionResult | None[源代码]

Extend a single seed bidirectionally.

参数:

  • query -- Query sequence or SequenceRecord.

  • seed -- Seed to extend.

  • subject_sequences -- Dictionary mapping subject ID to sequence.

返回:

ExtensionResult if successful, None otherwise.

extend_seeds(query: SequenceRecord | str, seeds: List[Seed], subject_sequences: Dict[str, str], max_results: int | None = None, progress: bool = True) → List[ExtensionResult][源代码]

Extend multiple seeds and return the best results.

参数:

  • query -- Query sequence or SequenceRecord.

  • seeds -- List of seeds to extend.

  • subject_sequences -- Dictionary mapping subject ID to sequence.

  • max_results -- Maximum number of results to return.

  • progress -- Show progress feedback.

返回:

List of ExtensionResult objects sorted by score.

blastmini.extension.evaluate_extension_result(result: ExtensionResult) → Dict[str, float][源代码]

Evaluate the quality of an extension result.

参数:

result -- ExtensionResult to evaluate.

返回:

Dictionary with quality metrics.

blastmini.extension.is_high_quality_extension(result: ExtensionResult, min_score: int | None = None, min_identity: float | None = None, min_length: int | None = None) → bool[源代码]

Check if an extension meets quality thresholds.

参数:

  • result -- ExtensionResult to check.

  • min_score -- Minimum score threshold.

  • min_identity -- Minimum identity percentage threshold.

  • min_length -- Minimum alignment length threshold.

返回:

True if the extension meets all thresholds.

blastmini.extension.main()[源代码]

Simple command line interface for testing the extension module.

blastmini.index module

k-mer index construction and management for blastmini.

This module provides hash-based k-mer indexing for nucleotide sequences, which is the foundation of the seed-and-extend search strategy. The index maps each k-mer to all positions where it appears in the database, enabling O(1) lookup during seed search.

Key features:

  • Canonical k-mer handling (considers reverse complement)

  • Memory-efficient indexing with position tracking

  • Index persistence (save/load JSON)

  • Support for multiple sequences in a single index

  • k-mer frequency analysis

class blastmini.index.IndexStats(total_kmers: int = 0, unique_kmers: int = 0, total_sequences: int = 0, total_length: int = 0, kmer_size: int = 0, memory_estimate_mb: float = 0.0)[源代码]

基类：object

Statistics about a k-mer index.

total_kmers

Total number of k-mer occurrences.

Type:

int

unique_kmers

Number of unique k-mers.

Type:

int

total_sequences

Number of sequences in the database.

Type:

int

total_length

Total sequence length.

Type:

int

kmer_size

Size of k-mers used.

Type:

int

memory_estimate_mb

Estimated memory usage in megabytes.

Type:

float

kmer_size: int = 0
memory_estimate_mb: float = 0.0
total_kmers: int = 0
total_length: int = 0
total_sequences: int = 0
unique_kmers: int = 0
class blastmini.index.KmerIndex(k: int = 11)[源代码]

基类：object

Hash-based k-mer index for nucleotide sequences.

This class manages the construction, querying, and persistence of k-mer indices. It supports canonical k-mers for strand-independent searching and provides statistics for understanding index properties.

index

Dictionary mapping k-mer string to list of (subject_id, position).

k

k-mer size used for the index.

stats

Index statistics.

subject_ids

List of sequence IDs in the database.

示例

>>> from blastmini.io import parse_fasta
>>> records = list(parse_fasta("database.fa"))
>>> index = KmerIndex.build(records, k=11)
>>> matches = index.lookup("ATCGATCGATC")
>>> print(f"Found {len(matches)} matches")
classmethod build(records: List[SequenceRecord], k: int = 11, canonical: bool = True, min_kmer_occurrences: int = 1, max_kmer_occurrences: int | None = None, progress: bool = True) → KmerIndex[源代码]

Build a k-mer index from a list of sequence records.

This is the primary factory method for creating indices. It handles filtering of repetitive k-mers and provides progress feedback.

参数:

  • records -- List of SequenceRecord objects to index.

  • k -- k-mer size.

  • canonical -- Use canonical k-mers (consider reverse complement).

  • min_kmer_occurrences -- Minimum occurrences to keep (filter rare).

  • max_kmer_occurrences -- Maximum occurrences to keep (filter repetitive).

  • progress -- Show progress bar (if tqdm is installed).

返回:

KmerIndex object with populated index.

示例

>>> from blastmini.models import SequenceRecord
>>> records = [SequenceRecord("S1", "ATCGATCG"), SequenceRecord("S2", "GATCGATC")]
>>> idx = KmerIndex.build(records, k=3)
>>> print(idx.lookup("ATC"))
[('S1', 0), ('S1', 4), ('S2', 2)]
classmethod build_from_fasta(fasta_file: str | PathLike, k: int = 11, canonical: bool = True, min_kmer_occurrences: int = 1, max_kmer_occurrences: int | None = None, max_records: int | None = None, progress: bool = True) → KmerIndex[源代码]

Build a k-mer index directly from a FASTA file.

Convenience method that parses the FASTA file and builds the index in one step. For large files, this uses streaming but still loads all records into memory.

参数:

  • fasta_file -- Path to FASTA file.

  • k -- k-mer size.

  • canonical -- Use canonical k-mers.

  • min_kmer_occurrences -- Filter rare k-mers.

  • max_kmer_occurrences -- Filter repetitive k-mers.

  • max_records -- Maximum number of records to index (for testing).

  • progress -- Show progress feedback.

返回:

KmerIndex object.

示例

>>> idx = KmerIndex.build_from_fasta("database.fa", k=11, max_records=100)
contains(kmer: str, canonical: bool = True) → bool[源代码]

Check if a k-mer exists in the index.

参数:

  • kmer -- k-mer string.

  • canonical -- Use canonical k-mers.

返回:

True if the k-mer is in the index.

coverage_stats() → Dict[str, float][源代码]

Calculate coverage statistics for the indexed sequences.

返回:

  • 'mean_coverage': Average number of k-mers covering each position.

  • 'coverage_std': Standard deviation of coverage.

  • 'zero_coverage_positions': Number of positions with no coverage.

返回类型:

Dictionary with coverage metrics

备注

This is a simplified estimate and may be slow for large indices.

get_kmers_with_counts(min_count: int = 1, max_count: int | None = None) → List[Tuple[str, int]][源代码]

Get k-mers with their occurrence counts.

Useful for analyzing k-mer distributions and filtering.

参数:

  • min_count -- Minimum count threshold.

  • max_count -- Maximum count threshold.

返回:

List of (kmer, count) tuples sorted by count descending.

classmethod load(filepath: str | PathLike) → KmerIndex[源代码]

Load index from JSON file.

参数:

filepath -- Path to JSON index file.

返回:

KmerIndex object.

抛出:

FileNotFoundError -- If file doesn't exist.

lookup(kmer: str, canonical: bool = True) → List[Tuple[str, int]][源代码]

Look up a k-mer in the index.

参数:

  • kmer -- k-mer string to look up.

  • canonical -- If True, lookup the canonical version.

返回:

List of (subject_id, position) tuples for the k-mer. Returns empty list if k-mer is not found.

示例

>>> idx = KmerIndex.build(records, k=3)
>>> idx.lookup("ATC")
[('S1', 0), ('S1', 4)]
lookup_batch(kmers: List[str], canonical: bool = True) → Dict[str, List[Tuple[str, int]]][源代码]

Look up multiple k-mers efficiently.

参数:

  • kmers -- List of k-mer strings.

  • canonical -- Use canonical k-mers.

返回:

Dictionary mapping k-mer string to list of matches.

prune(max_occurrences: int) → KmerIndex[源代码]

Remove highly repetitive k-mers from the index.

This is useful for reducing memory usage and improving search speed by excluding common repetitive elements.

参数:

max_occurrences -- Maximum occurrences to keep.

返回:

New KmerIndex with pruned index.

示例

>>> idx = KmerIndex.build(records, k=3)
>>> pruned = idx.prune(max_occurrences=10)
save(filepath: str | PathLike) → None[源代码]

Save index to JSON file.

This is a wrapper around io.save_index that includes metadata.

参数:

filepath -- Path to output JSON file.

blastmini.index.build_index_from_fasta(fasta_file: str | PathLike, k: int = 11, canonical: bool = True, min_occurrences: int = 1, max_occurrences: int | None = None, output_file: str | PathLike | None = None, progress: bool = True) → KmerIndex[源代码]

Build a k-mer index from a FASTA file.

This is a high-level convenience function that handles the complete index building workflow from a FASTA file.

参数:

  • fasta_file -- Path to FASTA file.

  • k -- k-mer size.

  • canonical -- Use canonical k-mers.

  • min_occurrences -- Minimum occurrences to keep.

  • max_occurrences -- Maximum occurrences to keep (prune repetitive).

  • output_file -- If provided, save index to this file.

  • progress -- Show progress feedback.

返回:

KmerIndex object.

示例

>>> idx = build_index_from_fasta("database.fa", k=11, output_file="index.json")
blastmini.index.canonical_kmer(kmer: str) → str[源代码]

Return the canonical representation of a k-mer.

The canonical k-mer is the lexicographically smaller of the k-mer and its reverse complement. This ensures that both strands of DNA map to the same index key, improving sensitivity.

参数:

kmer -- DNA k-mer string.

返回:

Canonical k-mer string.

示例

>>> canonical_kmer("ATCG")
'ATCG'
>>> canonical_kmer("CGAT")  # reverse complement of ATCG is ATCG
'ATCG'
>>> canonical_kmer("AAAA")
'AAAA'
blastmini.index.extract_kmers(sequence: str, k: int, canonical: bool = True) → Iterator[Tuple[str, int]][源代码]

Extract all k-mers from a sequence with their positions.

参数:

  • sequence -- DNA sequence string.

  • k -- k-mer length.

  • canonical -- If True, convert to canonical k-mers.

生成器:

Tuple of (kmer_string, position) for each k-mer.

示例

>>> list(extract_kmers("ATCGATCG", 3))
[('ATC', 0), ('TCG', 1), ('CGA', 2), ('GAT', 3), ('ATC', 4)]
blastmini.index.load_index_from_file(filepath: str | PathLike) → KmerIndex[源代码]

Load a k-mer index from a file.

Wrapper around KmerIndex.load.

参数:

filepath -- Path to index file.

返回:

KmerIndex object.

blastmini.index.main()[源代码]

Simple command line interface for testing the index module.

This allows building and inspecting indices from the command line.

blastmini.index.reverse_complement(seq: str) → str[源代码]

Compute the reverse complement of a DNA sequence.

参数:

seq -- DNA sequence string (A, T, C, G, N).

返回:

Reverse complement sequence.

示例

>>> reverse_complement("ATCG")
'CGAT'
>>> reverse_complement("N")
'N'

blastmini.io module

Input/Output operations for blastmini.

This module provides FASTA file parsing, index persistence, and result output functions. All parsers are implemented from scratch without external dependencies like Biopython.

blastmini.io.count_sequences(filepath: str | PathLike) → int[源代码]

Count number of sequences in a FASTA file.

This is a lightweight function that only counts headers without storing sequence data.

参数:

filepath -- Path to FASTA file.

返回:

Number of sequences in the file.

示例

>>> count = count_sequences("database.fa")
>>> print(f"Database contains {count} sequences")
blastmini.io.get_sequence_stats(filepath: str | PathLike) → Dict[str, Any][源代码]

Get summary statistics for a FASTA file.

Computes basic statistics including number of sequences, total length, min/max/mean length, and GC content.

参数:

filepath -- Path to FASTA file.

返回:

Dictionary with summary statistics.

示例

>>> stats = get_sequence_stats("database.fa")
>>> print(f"Total length: {stats['total_length']}")
blastmini.io.load_hits_from_tsv(input_file: str | PathLike) → List[Hit][源代码]

Load hits from a TSV file generated by save_hits_to_tsv.

参数:

input_file -- Path to TSV file.

返回:

List of Hit objects.

抛出:

ValueError -- If file format is invalid.

blastmini.io.load_index(index_file: str | PathLike) → Dict[str, List[tuple]][源代码]

Load k-mer index from JSON file.

参数:

index_file -- Path to JSON index file.

返回:

Dictionary mapping k-mer string to list of (subject_id, position).

示例

>>> index = load_index("index.json")
>>> print(len(index))
12345
blastmini.io.parse_fasta(filepath: str | PathLike) → Iterator[SequenceRecord][源代码]

Parse FASTA file and yield SequenceRecord objects.

blastmini.io.parse_fasta_multi(filepath: str | PathLike) → List[SequenceRecord][源代码]

Parse FASTA file and return all records as a list.

Convenience function for small files where streaming isn't necessary.

警告

For large files, use parse_fasta() iterator instead to avoid memory issues.

参数:

filepath -- Path to FASTA file.

返回:

List of all SequenceRecord objects in the file.

示例

>>> records = parse_fasta_multi("small.fa")
>>> print(len(records))
5
blastmini.io.save_hits_to_tsv(hits: List[Hit], output_file: str | PathLike) → None[源代码]

Save hit list to TSV file with header.

参数:

  • hits -- List of Hit objects.

  • output_file -- Path to output TSV file.

示例

>>> hits = [Hit(query_id="Q1", subject_id="S1", score=100)]
>>> save_hits_to_tsv(hits, "results.tsv")
blastmini.io.save_index(index: Dict[str, List[tuple]], index_file: str | PathLike) → None[源代码]

Save k-mer index to JSON file.

参数:

  • index -- Dictionary mapping k-mer string to list of (subject_id, position).

  • index_file -- Path to output JSON file.

备注

The index is serialized as JSON where keys are k-mers and values are lists of [subject_id, position] arrays.

示例

>>> index = {"ATCG": [("S1", 0), ("S2", 10)]}
>>> save_index(index, "index.json")

blastmini.models module

Data models for blastmini.

This module defines the core data structures used throughout the blastmini package, including sequence records, alignment hits, and configuration parameters.

class blastmini.models.AlignmentConfig(kmer_size: int = 11, match_score: int = 5, mismatch_penalty: int = -4, gap_open_penalty: int = -5, gap_extend_penalty: int = -2, x_dropoff: int = 5, top_n: int = 10, min_score: int = 0, min_identity: float = 0.0)[源代码]

基类：object

Configuration parameters for alignment algorithms.

This class centralizes all scoring and alignment parameters to avoid passing many individual arguments to functions.

kmer_size

Size of k-mers used for seeding (default: 11).

Type:

int

match_score

Score for matching nucleotides (default: 5).

Type:

int

mismatch_penalty

Score for mismatching nucleotides (negative, default: -4).

Type:

int

gap_open_penalty

Penalty for opening a gap (negative, default: -5).

Type:

int

gap_extend_penalty

Penalty for extending a gap (negative, default: -2).

Type:

int

x_dropoff

X-dropoff threshold for extension termination (default: 5).

Type:

int

top_n

Number of top hits to report (default: 10).

Type:

int

min_score

Minimum score to report a hit (default: 0).

Type:

int

min_identity

Minimum identity percentage to report (default: 0.0).

Type:

float

示例

>>> config = AlignmentConfig(kmer_size=15, match_score=1, mismatch_penalty=-1)
>>> print(config.kmer_size)
15
gap_extend_penalty: int = -2
gap_open_penalty: int = -5
kmer_size: int = 11
match_score: int = 5
min_identity: float = 0.0
min_score: int = 0
mismatch_penalty: int = -4
top_n: int = 10
x_dropoff: int = 5
class blastmini.models.Hit(query_id: str, subject_id: str, score: int = 0, identity_percent: float = 0.0, alignment_length: int = 0, query_start: int = -1, query_end: int = -1, subject_start: int = -1, subject_end: int = -1, query_alignment: str = '', subject_alignment: str = '', evalue: float | None = None, bit_score: float | None = None)[源代码]

基类：object

A single alignment hit between a query and a subject sequence.

This class stores all information about a seed-and-extend alignment result, including coordinates, scores, and alignment details.

query_id

Identifier of the query sequence.

Type:

str

subject_id

Identifier of the subject (database) sequence.

Type:

str

score

Raw alignment score from the extension process.

Type:

int

identity_percent

Percentage of identical residues in the alignment.

Type:

float

alignment_length

Total length of the alignment region.

Type:

int

query_start

Start position in the query sequence (0-based).

Type:

int

query_end

End position in the query sequence (0-based, exclusive).

Type:

int

subject_start

Start position in the subject sequence (0-based).

Type:

int

subject_end

End position in the subject sequence (0-based, exclusive).

Type:

int

query_alignment

The query sequence segment with gaps (if any).

Type:

str

subject_alignment

The subject sequence segment with gaps (if any).

Type:

str

evalue

Estimated E-value for this hit (if computed).

Type:

float | None

bit_score

Bit score for this hit (if computed).

Type:

float | None

示例

>>> hit = Hit(query_id="Q1", subject_id="S1", score=45, identity_percent=95.0)
>>> print(hit)
Hit(query='Q1', subject='S1', score=45, identity=95.0%)
alignment_length: int = 0
bit_score: float | None = None
evalue: float | None = None
identity_percent: float = 0.0
query_alignment: str = ''
query_end: int = -1
query_id: str
query_start: int = -1
score: int = 0
subject_alignment: str = ''
subject_end: int = -1
subject_id: str
subject_start: int = -1
to_tsv() → str[源代码]

Convert hit to tab-separated values for output.

返回:

A tab-separated string suitable for TSV output files.

示例

>>> hit = Hit(query_id="Q1", subject_id="S1", score=100)
>>> print(hit.to_tsv())
Q1  S1      100     ...
classmethod tsv_header() → str[源代码]

Return the TSV header line for hit output.

返回:

A tab-separated header string.

class blastmini.models.SequenceRecord(id: str, sequence: str, description: str | None = '')[源代码]

基类：object

A biological sequence record with identifier and sequence data.

This class represents a single sequence entry from a FASTA file or other sequence data source. It is designed to be immutable and hashable for use in indices and caches.

id

Unique sequence identifier (without the '>' character).

Type:

str

sequence

The biological sequence string (DNA/RNA/protein).

Type:

str

description

Optional description line from the FASTA header.

Type:

str | None

示例

>>> record = SequenceRecord("NC_000001", "ATCGATCGATCG")
>>> print(record.id)
NC_000001
description: str | None = ''
id: str
sequence: str

blastmini.scoring module

Scoring and ranking module for blastmini.

This module provides scoring functions for evaluating alignment quality, ranking hits by significance, calculating identity percentages, and formatting results for output.

Key features:

  • Hit scoring and ranking

  • Identity and similarity calculations

  • Score normalization (bitscore calculation)

  • Statistical score estimation (empirical)

  • Hit filtering by score thresholds

  • TSV/CSV output formatting

  • Alignment visualization

class blastmini.scoring.HitScorer(config: AlignmentConfig | None = None, matrix: Dict[str, Dict[str, int]] = {'A': {'A': 5, 'C': -4, 'G': -4, 'N': -1, 'T': -4}, 'C': {'A': -4, 'C': 5, 'G': -4, 'N': -1, 'T': -4}, 'G': {'A': -4, 'C': -4, 'G': 5, 'N': -1, 'T': -4}, 'N': {'A': -1, 'C': -1, 'G': -1, 'N': 0, 'T': -1}, 'T': {'A': -4, 'C': -4, 'G': -4, 'N': -1, 'T': 5}}, min_score: int = 0, min_identity: float = 0.0, top_n: int = 10)[源代码]

基类：object

Score and rank hits from extension results.

This class handles the scoring, ranking, and filtering of hits, including bit score calculation and E-value estimation.

config

Alignment configuration.

matrix

Scoring matrix to use.

min_score

Minimum score threshold.

min_identity

Minimum identity threshold.

top_n

Number of top hits to keep.

merge_hits(hits: List[Hit], subject_sequences: Dict[str, str] | None = None) → List[Hit][源代码]

Merge overlapping hits on the same subject.

参数:

  • hits -- List of Hit objects.

  • subject_sequences -- Dictionary mapping subject ID to sequence.

返回:

List of merged Hit objects.

score_hit(hit: Hit, query_length: int) → ScoredHit[源代码]

Calculate all scoring metrics for a single hit.

参数:

  • hit -- Hit object to score.

  • query_length -- Length of the query sequence.

返回:

ScoredHit with all metrics calculated.

score_hits(hits: List[Hit], query_length: int, db_total_length: int | None = None, progress: bool = True) → List[ScoredHit][源代码]

Score and rank a list of hits.

参数:

  • hits -- List of Hit objects.

  • query_length -- Length of the query sequence.

  • db_total_length -- Total length of the database (for E-value).

  • progress -- Show progress feedback.

返回:

List of ScoredHit objects sorted by score.

class blastmini.scoring.ScoredHit(hit: Hit, raw_score: int = 0, bit_score: float = 0.0, evalue: float = 1.0, identity_percent: float = 0.0, similarity_percent: float = 0.0, coverage_percent: float = 0.0, rank: int = 0, normalized_score: float = 0.0)[源代码]

基类：object

A hit with additional scoring information.

This extends the basic Hit with scoring metrics for ranking.

hit

The original Hit object.

Type:

blastmini.models.Hit

raw_score

Raw alignment score.

Type:

int

bit_score

Bit score (normalized).

Type:

float

evalue

E-value (statistical significance).

Type:

float

identity_percent

Percentage of identical characters.

Type:

float

similarity_percent

Percentage of similar characters.

Type:

float

coverage_percent

Percentage of query covered.

Type:

float

rank

Ranking position among hits.

Type:

int

normalized_score

Score normalized by alignment length.

Type:

float

bit_score: float = 0.0
coverage_percent: float = 0.0
evalue: float = 1.0
hit: Hit
identity_percent: float = 0.0
normalized_score: float = 0.0
rank: int = 0
raw_score: int = 0
similarity_percent: float = 0.0
to_dict() → Dict[str, Any][源代码]

Convert to dictionary for JSON output.

blastmini.scoring.calculate_alignment_score(query_alignment: str, subject_alignment: str, matrix: Dict[str, Dict[str, int]] = {'A': {'A': 5, 'C': -4, 'G': -4, 'N': -1, 'T': -4}, 'C': {'A': -4, 'C': 5, 'G': -4, 'N': -1, 'T': -4}, 'G': {'A': -4, 'C': -4, 'G': 5, 'N': -1, 'T': -4}, 'N': {'A': -1, 'C': -1, 'G': -1, 'N': 0, 'T': -1}, 'T': {'A': -4, 'C': -4, 'G': -4, 'N': -1, 'T': 5}}) → Tuple[int, int, int][源代码]

Calculate score, identity, and mismatches from an alignment.

参数:

  • query_alignment -- Aligned query sequence (may contain gaps).

  • subject_alignment -- Aligned subject sequence (may contain gaps).

  • matrix -- Scoring matrix to use.

返回:

Tuple of (score, identity_count, mismatches_count).

示例

>>> score, identity, mismatches = calculate_alignment_score("A-T", "A-C")
>>> print(score, identity, mismatches)
1 1 1
blastmini.scoring.format_hit_as_text(scored: ScoredHit, show_alignment: bool = True) → str[源代码]

Format a scored hit as human-readable text.

参数:

  • scored -- ScoredHit object.

  • show_alignment -- Whether to show the alignment.

返回:

Formatted string.

示例

>>> print(format_hit_as_text(scored))
Rank: 1
Subject: S1
Score: 145 (bitscore: 50.0)
E-value: 1.23e-05
Identity: 98.5%
Alignment: 200/200
blastmini.scoring.format_hits_as_bed(scored_hits: List[ScoredHit]) → str[源代码]

Format hits as BED (Browser Extensible Data) format.

参数:

scored_hits -- List of ScoredHit objects.

返回:

BED formatted string.

blastmini.scoring.format_hits_as_json(scored_hits: List[ScoredHit]) → str[源代码]

Format hits as JSON.

参数:

scored_hits -- List of ScoredHit objects.

返回:

JSON formatted string.

blastmini.scoring.format_hits_as_text(scored_hits: List[ScoredHit], top_n: int = 10) → str[源代码]

Format multiple hits as human-readable text.

参数:

  • scored_hits -- List of ScoredHit objects.

  • top_n -- Number of top hits to show.

返回:

Formatted string.

blastmini.scoring.format_hits_as_tsv(scored_hits: List[ScoredHit]) → str[源代码]

Format hits as TSV with header matching Hit.tsv_header.

The column order is: query_id, subject_id, score, identity_percent, alignment_length, query_start, query_end, subject_start, subject_end, evalue, bit_score.

blastmini.scoring.get_nucleotide_score(char1: str, char2: str, matrix: Dict[str, Dict[str, int]] = {'A': {'A': 5, 'C': -4, 'G': -4, 'N': -1, 'T': -4}, 'C': {'A': -4, 'C': 5, 'G': -4, 'N': -1, 'T': -4}, 'G': {'A': -4, 'C': -4, 'G': 5, 'N': -1, 'T': -4}, 'N': {'A': -1, 'C': -1, 'G': -1, 'N': 0, 'T': -1}, 'T': {'A': -4, 'C': -4, 'G': -4, 'N': -1, 'T': 5}}) → int[源代码]

Get score for a pair of nucleotides.

参数:

  • char1 -- First nucleotide character.

  • char2 -- Second nucleotide character.

  • matrix -- Scoring matrix to use.

返回:

Score for the pair.

示例

>>> get_nucleotide_score('A', 'A')
5
>>> get_nucleotide_score('A', 'T')
-4
blastmini.scoring.main()[源代码]

Simple command line interface for testing scoring.

blastmini.scoring.visualize_alignment(query_alignment: str, subject_alignment: str, line_width: int = 60, show_consensus: bool = True) → str[源代码]

Create a formatted alignment visualization.

参数:

  • query_alignment -- Aligned query sequence.

  • subject_alignment -- Aligned subject sequence.

  • line_width -- Number of characters per line.

  • show_consensus -- Show consensus line (| for match, . for mismatch).

返回:

Formatted alignment string.

示例

>>> print(visualize_alignment("ATCGATCG", "ATCGATCG"))
Query:   ATCGATCG
         ||||||||
Subject: ATCGATCG

blastmini.seeding module

Seed search module for blastmini.

This module implements the seeding phase of the BLAST algorithm, which is responsible for finding initial matches (seeds) between query sequences and the indexed database. The seeding phase is critical for efficiency as it reduces the search space from O(mn) to O(m * k_mer_matches).

Key features:

  • Query k-mer extraction with position tracking

  • Dictionary-based seed finding using the k-mer index

  • Seed clustering (merge nearby seeds)

  • Seed filtering based on score and position

  • Support for single and multiple query sequences

  • Progress tracking for large queries

class blastmini.seeding.Seed(query_id: str, subject_id: str, query_pos: int, subject_pos: int, kmer: str = '', score: int = 0, strand: str = '+')[源代码]

基类：object

A single seed match between query and subject sequences.

A seed represents a k-mer match that serves as a starting point for the extension phase.

query_id

Identifier of the query sequence.

Type:

str

subject_id

Identifier of the subject sequence.

Type:

str

query_pos

Position in the query sequence (0-based).

Type:

int

subject_pos

Position in the subject sequence (0-based).

Type:

int

kmer

The k-mer that formed this seed.

Type:

str

score

Initial seed score (default 0, can be used for filtering).

Type:

int

strand

'+' for same strand, '-' for reverse complement.

Type:

str

示例

>>> seed = Seed("Q1", "S1", 10, 100, "ATCGAT")
>>> print(seed)
Seed(Q1 -> S1 at query=10, subject=100)
distance_to(other: Seed) → int[源代码]

Calculate the distance between two seeds on the query axis.

参数:

other -- Another seed to compare with.

返回:

Absolute distance in query positions.

is_on_same_diagonal(other: Seed, max_offset: int = 10) → bool[源代码]

Check if two seeds lie on the same diagonal.

Two seeds are on the same diagonal if the difference between their query and subject positions is similar (allowing some offset).

参数:

  • other -- Another seed to compare with.

  • max_offset -- Maximum allowed offset in diagonal difference.

返回:

True if seeds are on the same diagonal.

kmer: str = ''
query_id: str
query_pos: int
score: int = 0
strand: str = '+'
subject_id: str
subject_pos: int
class blastmini.seeding.SeedCluster(query_id: str, subject_id: str, seeds: ~typing.List[~blastmini.seeding.Seed] = <factory>, strand: str = '+')[源代码]

基类：object

A cluster of nearby seeds on the same diagonal.

Clustering seeds helps identify regions with multiple matches, which increases confidence and improves extension quality.

query_id

Query sequence ID.

Type:

str

subject_id

Subject sequence ID.

Type:

str

seeds

List of seeds in the cluster.

Type:

List[blastmini.seeding.Seed]

strand

'+' or '-' for the strand orientation.

Type:

str

query_range

(start, end) of the cluster on the query.

subject_range

(start, end) of the cluster on the subject.

total_score

Sum of all seed scores.

density

Number of seeds per unit length.

示例

>>> cluster = SeedCluster("Q1", "S1")
>>> cluster.add_seed(Seed("Q1", "S1", 10, 100))
>>> cluster.add_seed(Seed("Q1", "S1", 15, 105))
>>> print(cluster)
SeedCluster(Q1 -> S1: 2 seeds, range q=10-15, s=100-105)
add_seed(seed: Seed) → None[源代码]

Add a seed to the cluster.

max_score_seed() → Seed | None[源代码]

Return the seed with the highest score in the cluster.

query_id: str
seeds: List[Seed]
strand: str = '+'
subject_id: str
class blastmini.seeding.SeedFinder(index: KmerIndex, config: AlignmentConfig | None = None, canonical: bool = True, min_seed_score: int = 1, max_seed_cluster_gap: int = 20, max_seeds_per_region: int = 100)[源代码]

基类：object

Finds seeds by matching query k-mers against the index.

This class implements the core seeding algorithm, including k-mer extraction, dictionary lookup, and seed clustering.

index

KmerIndex object containing the database.

config

AlignmentConfig with parameters.

canonical

Whether to use canonical k-mers.

min_seed_score

Minimum seed score to keep.

max_seed_cluster_gap

Maximum gap to cluster seeds together.

示例

>>> from blastmini.index import KmerIndex
>>> from blastmini.io import parse_fasta
>>> records = list(parse_fasta("database.fa"))
>>> idx = KmerIndex.build(records, k=11)
>>> finder = SeedFinder(idx)
>>> seeds = finder.find_seeds(records[0])
>>> print(f"Found {len(seeds)} seeds")
cluster_seeds(seeds: List[Seed]) → List[SeedCluster][源代码]

Cluster seeds by subject and diagonal.

Seeds on the same diagonal within the same subject are clustered together, which helps identify high-confidence regions.

参数:

seeds -- List of seeds to cluster.

返回:

List of SeedCluster objects.

find_best_seeds(query: SequenceRecord | str, top_n: int = 10, subject_ids: List[str] | None = None) → List[Seed][源代码]

Find the best seeds for a query, prioritizing high-density regions.

This method finds seeds, clusters them, and returns the top seeds from the best clusters.

参数:

  • query -- Query sequence or SequenceRecord.

  • top_n -- Number of top seeds to return.

  • subject_ids -- Optional subject IDs to search.

返回:

List of top seeds.

find_seeds(query: SequenceRecord | str, subject_ids: List[str] | None = None, max_seeds: int | None = None, progress: bool = True) → List[Seed][源代码]

Find seeds for a single query sequence.

参数:

  • query -- Query sequence or SequenceRecord.

  • subject_ids -- Optional list of subject IDs to search (all if None).

  • max_seeds -- Maximum number of seeds to return.

  • progress -- Show progress feedback.

返回:

List of Seed objects.

示例

>>> seeds = finder.find_seeds("ATCGATCGATCG")
>>> for seed in seeds[:5]:
...     print(seed)
find_seeds_batch(queries: List[SequenceRecord | str], subject_ids: List[str] | None = None, max_seeds_per_query: int | None = None, progress: bool = True) → Dict[str, List[Seed]][源代码]

Find seeds for multiple query sequences.

参数:

  • queries -- List of query sequences or SequenceRecords.

  • subject_ids -- Optional list of subject IDs to search.

  • max_seeds_per_query -- Maximum seeds per query.

  • progress -- Show progress feedback.

返回:

Dictionary mapping query ID to list of seeds.

blastmini.seeding.filter_seeds_by_coverage(seeds: List[Seed], max_coverage: int = 10) → List[Seed][源代码]

Filter seeds to avoid over-sampling highly covered regions.

参数:

  • seeds -- List of seeds.

  • max_coverage -- Maximum number of seeds per query position.

返回:

Filtered list of seeds.

blastmini.seeding.filter_seeds_by_density(seeds: List[Seed], min_density: float = 1.0, window_size: int = 100) → List[Seed][源代码]

Filter seeds to keep only those in high-density regions.

参数:

  • seeds -- List of seeds.

  • min_density -- Minimum density threshold (seeds per 100 bases).

  • window_size -- Window size for density calculation.

返回:

Filtered list of seeds.

blastmini.seeding.main()[源代码]

Simple command line interface for testing the seeding module.

blastmini.stats module

Statistical significance estimation for blastmini.

This module provides statistical significance estimation for sequence alignments, including E-value calculation and p-value estimation. Unlike real BLAST which uses Karlin-Altschul extreme value distribution theory, this implementation uses empirical permutation testing for educational purposes.

Key features:

  • Empirical p-value estimation via sequence permutation

  • E-value calculation from empirical distributions

  • Score distribution modeling (normal approximation)

  • Background score estimation from random sequences

  • Statistical significance reporting

  • Multiple testing correction (Bonferroni, FDR)

class blastmini.stats.ScoreDistribution(scores: ~typing.List[int] = <factory>, mean: float = 0.0, std: float = 0.0, min_score: int = 0, max_score: int = 0, n_samples: int = 0, fitted_params: ~typing.Dict[str, float] = <factory>)[源代码]

基类：object

Distribution of alignment scores from random sequences.

This class stores and analyzes score distributions for estimating statistical significance.

scores

List of observed scores.

Type:

List[int]

mean

Mean of the distribution.

Type:

float

std

Standard deviation of the distribution.

Type:

float

min_score

Minimum score observed.

Type:

int

max_score

Maximum score observed.

Type:

int

n_samples

Number of samples in the distribution.

Type:

int

fitted_params

Parameters for fitted distribution.

Type:

Dict[str, float]

示例

>>> dist = ScoreDistribution([10, 15, 12, 18, 11])
>>> print(f"Mean: {dist.mean:.2f}, Std: {dist.std:.2f}")
Mean: 13.20, Std: 3.03
add_score(score: int) → None[源代码]

Add a score to the distribution.

estimate_extreme_params() → Tuple[float, float][源代码]

Estimate parameters for extreme value distribution.

This is a simplified approximation for the Gumbel distribution parameters (λ and K) used in real BLAST.

返回:

Tuple of (lambda_param, K_param).

fit_normal() → Dict[str, float][源代码]

Fit a normal distribution to the scores.

返回:

Dictionary with 'mean' and 'std' parameters.

fitted_params: Dict[str, float]
get_evalue(score: int, effective_db_size: int) → float[源代码]

Estimate E-value for a given score.

E-value is the expected number of random hits with score >= given score.

参数:

  • score -- Score to evaluate.

  • effective_db_size -- Effective database size (total length).

返回:

Estimated E-value.

get_percentile(percentile: float) → int[源代码]

Get a percentile of the distribution.

参数:

percentile -- Percentile value (0-100).

返回:

Score at the given percentile.

get_pvalue(score: int) → float[源代码]

Estimate p-value for a given score.

P-value is the probability of observing a score >= given score in the background distribution.

参数:

score -- Score to evaluate.

返回:

Estimated p-value.

max_score: int = 0
mean: float = 0.0
min_score: int = 0
n_samples: int = 0
scores: List[int]
std: float = 0.0
class blastmini.stats.SignificanceEstimator(config: AlignmentConfig | None = None, n_permutations: int = 100, random_seed: int | None = None, min_score_threshold: int = 10)[源代码]

基类：object

Estimate statistical significance of sequence alignments.

This class uses empirical permutation testing to estimate the significance of alignment scores. It can also fit extreme value distributions for more efficient estimation.

config

Alignment configuration.

seed_extender

SeedExtender for generating random alignments.

hit_scorer

HitScorer for scoring alignments.

n_permutations

Number of permutations for empirical estimation.

random_seed

Random seed for reproducibility.

background_distribution

Score distribution from random sequences.

extreme_params

Fitted extreme value distribution parameters.

示例

>>> estimator = SignificanceEstimator(n_permutations=100)
>>> dist = estimator.estimate_background_distribution(query, db_sequences)
>>> result = estimator.estimate_significance(hit, query_length, db_size)
>>> print(f"E-value: {result.evalue:.2e}")
adjust_for_multiple_testing(results: List[SignificanceResult], method: str = 'bonferroni') → List[SignificanceResult][源代码]

Apply multiple testing correction.

参数:

  • results -- List of SignificanceResult objects.

  • method -- Correction method ('bonferroni' or 'fdr').

返回:

List of corrected SignificanceResult objects.

estimate_background_distribution(query: str | SequenceRecord, subject_sequences: Dict[str, str], n_permutations: int | None = None, progress: bool = True) → ScoreDistribution[源代码]

Estimate the background score distribution using permutation.

This method generates random alignments by permuting the query sequence and scoring alignments to get the background distribution.

参数:

  • query -- Query sequence or SequenceRecord.

  • subject_sequences -- Dictionary mapping subject ID to sequence.

  • n_permutations -- Number of permutations (uses default if None).

  • progress -- Show progress feedback.

返回:

ScoreDistribution object.

estimate_extreme_value_distribution(query: str | SequenceRecord, subject_sequences: Dict[str, str], n_permutations: int | None = None, progress: bool = True) → Tuple[float, float][源代码]

Estimate parameters for extreme value distribution.

This fits a Gumbel distribution to the extreme scores from permutation testing.

参数:

  • query -- Query sequence or SequenceRecord.

  • subject_sequences -- Dictionary mapping subject ID to sequence.

  • n_permutations -- Number of permutations.

  • progress -- Show progress feedback.

返回:

Tuple of (lambda_param, K_param).

estimate_significance(hit: Hit, query_length: int, db_size: int, use_extreme_distribution: bool = True) → SignificanceResult[源代码]

Estimate significance of a single hit.

参数:

  • hit -- Hit object to evaluate.

  • query_length -- Length of the query sequence.

  • db_size -- Total length of the database.

  • use_extreme_distribution -- Use extreme value distribution if available.

返回:

SignificanceResult object.

示例

>>> result = estimator.estimate_significance(hit, 1000, 5000000)
>>> if result.is_significant:
...     print("Significant hit found!")
estimate_significance_batch(hits: List[Hit], query_length: int, db_size: int, use_extreme_distribution: bool = True, progress: bool = True) → List[SignificanceResult][源代码]

Estimate significance for multiple hits.

参数:

  • hits -- List of Hit objects.

  • query_length -- Length of the query sequence.

  • db_size -- Total length of the database.

  • use_extreme_distribution -- Use extreme value distribution if available.

  • progress -- Show progress feedback.

返回:

List of SignificanceResult objects.

class blastmini.stats.SignificanceResult(hit: Hit, raw_score: int, pvalue: float = 1.0, evalue: float = 1.0, bit_score: float = 0.0, lambda_param: float = 0.1, K_param: float = 0.1, is_significant: bool = False, significance_level: float = 0.05)[源代码]

基类：object

Statistical significance results for a hit.

hit

The original hit.

Type:

blastmini.models.Hit

raw_score

Raw alignment score.

Type:

int

pvalue

Estimated p-value.

Type:

float

evalue

Estimated E-value.

Type:

float

bit_score

Bit score (normalized).

Type:

float

lambda_param

Lambda parameter from extreme value distribution.

Type:

float

K_param

K parameter from extreme value distribution.

Type:

float

is_significant

Whether the hit is statistically significant.

Type:

bool

significance_level

Significance level used.

Type:

float

示例

>>> result = SignificanceResult(hit, 100, 1e-5, 0.001)
>>> print(f"E-value: {result.evalue:.2e}, Significant: {result.is_significant}")
E-value: 1.00e-03, Significant: True
K_param: float = 0.1
bit_score: float = 0.0
evalue: float = 1.0
hit: Hit
is_significant: bool = False
lambda_param: float = 0.1
pvalue: float = 1.0
raw_score: int
significance_level: float = 0.05
to_dict() → Dict[str, Any][源代码]

Convert to dictionary for output.

blastmini.stats.format_significance_results(results: List[SignificanceResult], format: str = 'text') → str[源代码]

Format significance results for output.

参数:

  • results -- List of SignificanceResult objects.

  • format -- Output format ('text', 'tsv', 'json').

返回:

Formatted string.
